_doc: Editor steering notes for the conference preview agent. Authoritative — these override prior knowledge and any conflicting context-data interpretation. Keep each note to 1–3 short sentences. Empty strings are dropped silently. Loaded by build_conference_context.py from /cfb-research/writer_notes/<season>/<conf>.json.
_usage: conference_note: 1–3 sentences with two purposes — (a) the meta-narrative for the year (e.g., 'The realignment dust has settled but the talent gap between the top three and the rest is still widening.'), and (b) calibration the agent needs to right-size confidence: what 'good' looks like in this league. SEC example: 'In modern SEC: 7-1 in conf typically reaches the title game; 9-0 has never happened. 9 overall wins is a historic season.' MAC example: 'In the MAC, 8 conference wins is championship caliber; 6-2 contends.' Combine narrative + calibration when both matter.

team_notes.<slug>: 1–3 sentences about that specific team — what the data won't tell the agent (a coaching change, a critical transfer, an injury, an off-field story, the angle the writeup should take).
Slugs match the canonical team slugs in build_team_context.py / scrape_team_context.py.
conference_note: The newly reformed Pac 12 conference will have 8 total members in 2026, 6 new plus holdovers Oregon State and Washington State who were the only members in 2025 & 2024.  This new Pac 12 wants to establish themselves as the premier G6 conference, and will likely try to add new members to boost their profile in years to come.  Three storylines define the 2026 Pac 12: (1) Can this newly rebuilt conference with six new members recapture the magic of late night madness formerly known as #Pac12AfterDark? (2) Does Boise State run this new Pac 12 like they did in the Mountain West?  (3) Or can San Diego State, Fresno State and/or Texas State establish themselves as Boise State's equal in the Pac 12 upper class?  Pac 12 Tier framework: When team_notes give a 3-tier range (e.g., Tier 2-4), it's due to this new Pac 12 being more unpredictable than most due to a new conference sorting itself out. The agent should NOT over-anchor on a single 'centering' tier; instead treat the range itself as the projection. A team at Tier 2-4 means 'either it works and they're contending for the title game or it doesn't and they're fighting for a bowl' — both branches are real. Conference USA tier framework (tiers describe team caliber, NOT exact record): Tier 1 = Pac 12 title-game favorite, lower tier CFB bowl game contenders ; Tier 2 = title-game contender, lower tier bowl game contenders; Tier 3 = middle tier and title-game and bowl game outsider; Tier 4 = bottom of the conference where reaching bowl eligibility would itself be a tremendous achievement. When team_notes give a range, the agent should treat the wider boundary as the realistic downside — but per the volatility note above, 3-tier ranges are common here and the range itself should be framed as the projection rather than collapsing to a single tier. The Pac 12 Championship game is played in the home stadium of the division leader with the best overall conference win percentage.

team_notes.boise-state: Tier 1 Pac 12 team. The Broncos won the MWC championship three straight years from 2023-2025. They are the initial favorite to win the Pac 12. QB Maddux Madsen back for his 3rd season starting; RBs Dylan Riley and Sire Gaines are the best duo in the Pac-12, with QB Madsen the best backfield.  OL returns 3 starters, likely the best OL in Pac 12; Defense is a good unit, let by DE Jayden Virgin-Morgan and LB Boen Phelps.  Replacing most secondary beyond safety Jaden Mickey. Boise has the 3rd largest football budget amoung all of the G6 teams.
team_notes.colorado-state: Tier 2-3 Pac 12 team. New HC Jim Mora is a good one from UConn but is rebuilding 75% of the entire roster; Mora brought 19 portal transfers from UConn with him, 40 total portal additions. Rams are the most experienced team in the Pac 12 due to all the portal starters added. QB will be one of three players battling for the starting QB1 job. OL will likely start 4 portal addtions, 2 from UConn; RB will need to emerge from 4 portal additions from UConn. WR has some options, TE Juice Vereen is a good one from UConn. DC Tyson Summers a good one has 16 portal additions to work with to rebuild a DL rotation and secondary.
team_notes.fresno-state: Tier 2 Pac 12 team. Won a MWC championship in 2022. HC Matt Entz in his second season after a good 9-4 debut in 2025; QB comptetion between Jayden Mandal and QB Khristian Martin (a portal add from Maryland) will be settled in fall camp but neither has much college experience. RB unit is strong with Bryson Donelson and Rayshon Luke returning along with good depth; OL returns 3 starters from a very strong group last season.  WR Josiah Freeman is a really good one.  Defense the much stronger unit has to replace some, but CB Simeon Harris and Safety Ryan Wilson to form a top looking Pac 12 secondary.  Bulldogs are one of the co-favorites in the Pac 12 behind Boise State.
team_notes.oregon-state: Tier 3 Pac 12 team. One of the two holdovers from the previous era of the Pac 12. New HC JaMarcus Shephard is a first time HC, previously working as the Associate head coach/Wide Receivers coach at Alabama. 60% of the roster is new, Beavers added 11 starters from the FCS level.  QB1 competition between last years starter Malik Murphy and portal addition Braden Atkinson will continue into fall camp; RBs must emerge, WRs top performers are from FCS level; OL has five with starting experience, two are from the FCS level.  Defensive Line was best unit in spring led by DE Takari Hickle.  LB Aiden Sullivan the top player on the team. Beavers project as a lower tier Pac 12 team.
team_notes.san-diego-state: Tier 2 Pac 12 team.  HC Sean Lewis in his third season, won 3 games in 2024 then 9 games in 2025. 2025 starting QB Jayden Denegal returns; RB Lucky Sutton was 1st team All MWC in 2025; WR Jordan Napier was 2nd team All MWC in 2025. OL has 5 with starting experience when you add in 3 portal starters. Good defense led by a secondary that projects as one of the best in the conference led by Safety Dalesean Staley. Aztecs are one of the co-favorites in the Pac 12 behind Boise State.
team_notes.texas-state: Tier 2-3 Pac 12 team. HC G. J. Kinne a good one is considered one of the best in the Pac 12 and the G6, will be in line for a P4 job in the near future.  QB Brad Jackson is one of, if not the best in the G6 and has two 1,000 yard WRs in Beau Sparks and Chris Dawn Jr.  Lead RB Greg Burrell was dismissed from the team, new RBs will need to emerge.  OL is rebuilding with only 2 starters returning.  Defense is the issue, new DC Will Windham was brought in from South Alabama to improve the unit along with 13 portal additions, including 5 starters from the FCS level.  Bobcats have a really fun offense that will make them a hard out, but defense will need to improve to contend in this new Pac 12 conference.
team_notes.utah-state: Tier 4 Pac 12 team. HC Bronco Mendenhall in his second season at Utah State after going 6-7 in 2025.  Aggies are one of the most experienced team in the Pac 12 due to 12 returning starters along with portal starters added. 70% of the roster is new with 32 players added in the transfer portal, 17 of which are from the P4 level. QB McCae Hillstead, a portal addition from BYU won the starting job; RB Javen Jacobs returns and WRs added some good portals like Javon Robinson from Georgia State.  OL has 3 returning starters and looks decent.  The defensive line added 8 portal players to rebuild around DTs Carson Tujague and Tyree Morris. This is an experienced looking defense that 2nd year DC Nick Howell should be able to improve.
team_notes.washington-state: Tier 3 Pac 12 team. One of the two holdovers from the previous era of the Pac 12. Least experienced roster in the Pac 12 with over 70% new. New HC Kirby Moore was the OC at Missouri, is a Washington State alumni. Three way QB competition for the starting job will continue in fall camp; RB Kirby Vorhees returns with some good depth at teh position.  WR Tony Freeman returns and 4 portal WRs added.  OL has two returning starters and lots of questions.  Defense the better unit with the DL projected as the strength of the team behind DE's Linus Zunk and Matyus McLain, the LB's and secondary have starting experience from other rosters.
